heroPoems.title: From Collapse to Clarity
heroPoems.firstStanza:
You grieve for those
who need no grieving.
The wise do not mourn
for living or dead.
There was never a time
when you,

heroPoems.title: Whispers of Immortality
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Webster was much possessed by death
And saw the skull beneath the skin;
And breastless creatures under ground
Leaned backward with a lipless grin.
heroPoems.tagline: For when the street goes quiet.

heroPoems.title: Time is endless in thy hands, my lord
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Time is endless in thy hands, my lord. There is none to count thy
minutes.
Days and nights pass and ages bloom and fade like flowers. Thou knowest
how to wait.
Thy centuries follow each other perfecting a small wild flower.
We have no time to lose, and having no time we must scramble for a
heroPoems.tagline: For slow hours with a warm cup.

heroPoems.title: The Power of the Dog
heroPoems.firstStanza:
There is sorrow enough in the natural way
From men and women to fill our day;
But when we are certain of sorrow in store,
Why do we always arrange for more?
_Brothers and Sisters, I bid you beware
Of giving your heart to a dog to tear._
heroPoems.tagline: For slow hours with a warm cup.

heroPoems.title: I do not call this love; call it obsession if you prefer.
heroPoems.firstStanza:
I do not call this love; call it obsession if you prefer.
Names do not alter the fever.

heroPoems.title: Success is counted sweetest
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Success is counted sweetest
By those who ne'er succeed.
To comprehend a nectar
Requires sorest need.
heroPoems.tagline: For when rain taps the window.

heroPoems.title: Leviathan Laughs
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Can you pull Leviathan out
with a hook?
He laughs at spears
as if they were straw.

heroPoems.title: Sea Fever
heroPoems.firstStanza:
I must go down to the seas again, to the lonely sea and the sky,
And all I ask is a tall ship and a star to steer her by;
And the wheel's kick and the wind's song and the white sail's shaking,
And a grey mist on the sea's face, and a grey dawn breaking.

heroPoems.title: Sonnets to Orpheus II, 13
heroPoems.firstStanza:
Be ahead of all parting,
as if it were already behind you,
like the winter that has just gone.
For among winters one is so endless
that, wintering through it,
your heart survives all seasons.
heroPoems.tagline: For slow hours with a warm cup.

Woe to unhappy Thames!

featuredPoem.title: On First Looking into Chapman's Homer
featuredPoem.firstLines:
Much have I travell'd in the realms of gold,
And many goodly states and kingdoms seen;

poems.title: Return and let courage enter this wounded heart
I taste a liquor never brewed
